sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports experiencing intermittent dizziness, particularly when standing, for approximately one week. He denies any chest pain, shortness of breath, or syncope. He reports taking ibuprofen as directed.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Blood pressure is 90/60 mmHg. Heart rate is 88 bpm, regular. Neurological exam reveals normal cranial nerves, strength, and coordination. Gait is steady.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient's symptoms are most consistent with orthostatic hypotension, potentially exacerbated by ibuprofen use.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: I will order a tilt table test to evaluate for orthostatic intolerance. I will also advise the patient to increase fluid intake and avoid rapid positional changes. I will counsel the patient on the potential side effects of ibuprofen and recommend alternative pain management strategies if necessary.
